explicit_text: I, Maria Camila Palacio, was born in 1992 in Bogotá, Colombia. In 2015, I moved to Chicago to pursue my master's degree at Loyola University Chicago. Like my grandparents, I have my own traveling stories and have met people from all over the world, which has enriched my life greatly. I look forward to returning home with many stories to share with future generations of my family.

explicit_text: My grandfather Alfonso Chivivi was a remarkable traveler who journeyed around the world. One of his most fascinating experiences was living in Buenos Aires as a personal guest of President Juan Domingo Perón after attending a state dinner. His clever gift of emeralds to Evita Perón earned him six months living at the government's expense, a story that has always amazed me.

explicit_text: My other grandfather, Eduardo Palacio, grew up in Ituango, a small town in Antioquia. At 14, he traveled by mule for four days to reach Medellín, where he was amazed by the city's modernity, especially cars. He earned scholarships to finish high school in Medellín and later to study at a university in Bogotá, where he met my grandmother.

explicit_text: Ituango is a small town in the mountains of Antioquia where my grandfather Eduardo Palacio was born and raised. When he was 14, he undertook a challenging four-day mule journey from Ituango to Medellín because there were no roads connecting the two places. Ituango represents the humble beginnings of my grandfather's inspiring journey.
